metadata.title:
SWARMlNG IN TO FILE THEIR
CLUTCHES ON WORKING
MEN BEFORE PROPOSED
REGULATIONS BECOME EF-
FECTiVE.

pages.raw_text:
The probability Of the passage
of the Cotterill bill regulating loan
sharks has caused haste among
those engaged in the business in
Spokane to get their assignments
of wages filed with employers be-
fore the bill is passed


The extent to which these
sharks operate in Spokane is
shown by the fact that 10 agencies
have thrived in the business of
making salary loans at an an
nounced rate of interest of 10 per
cent per month, but those who
have had occasion to patronize
these institutions say that often
times the rate is nearer % per
cent per month.


The loan shark makes his plan
of doing business appear innocent
enough on first inquiry, but once
the borrower gets into the clutches
of the loan shark 1t may take
death or an appeal to the bank
ruptcy court to secure his release.


In the three months ending
January 81, 20 salary assignments
have been made by employees of
the Great Northern railway, most
ly to the salary loan sharks. Since
February |, Is of these assign
ments have been filed, which indi-
Gates big increase in the filings
for fear of the operation of the
Cotterill bill.


This measure requires the sigma.
ture of employers to be made to
all salary assignments under $500,
and also requires the wife of the
party making the assignment of
wages to sign the same where the
amount Is under $500. Such as
signment must be filed within a
few days days after being made,
whereas at the present time the
